name: Goswami Gokulnatha
dates_notes: Fourth son of Viṭṭhalanātha and head of the fourth gaddī; sectarian chronicles plus near-contemporary court references.
core_thesis:
Gokulanātha is the post-Viṭṭhalanātha systematizer of *Puṣṭi-Mārga* internal discipline. The doctrinal architecture is preserved without alteration: Brahman-as-Kṛṣṇa as the sole reality (*Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa-Vivṛti* on Vallabha's *Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha*); *jīva* and *jagat* as real *aṃśas* through *āvirbhāva–tirobhāva* without *māyā*; *Puṣṭi* as divine grace through *anugraha*; the threefold *jīva*-typology (*puṣṭi*, *maryādā*, *pravāha*).

The contribution is in three registers. First, the *Vārtā* tradition: Gokulanātha is the principal redactor of the *Caurāsī-Vaiṣṇavan-kī-Vārtā* ("narratives of the eighty-four *vaiṣṇavas*") and *Do-Sau-Bāvan-Vaiṣṇavan-kī-Vārtā* ("narratives of the two-hundred-fifty-two *vaiṣṇavas*"), the Vraja-Bhāṣā hagiographical compendia that fix the lineage's memory of Vallabha's and Viṭṭhalanātha's first-generation disciples. The *Vārtās* are doctrinal in form: each disciple-narrative is structured to illustrate a specific *bhāva*-mode, a specific *seva*-difficulty, or a specific instance of *anugraha* operating against the *jīva*'s expected resistance (so the editorial *avatārikā* prefaces). Second, the *Vivṛti* on Vallabha's *Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha* — the *Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa-Vivṛti* — that opens Vallabha's compressed glosses on the metaphysics of *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* at scholastic pitch sufficient for the lineage's internal teaching but below the polemical pitch of Viṭṭhalanātha's *Vidvan-Maṇḍana* or Puruṣottama's *Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga*. Third, the consolidation of the *Saṃpradāya-Pradīpa* — the school's manual of liturgical and ritual structure for the *gaddī*-system that Viṭṭhalanātha had established — fixing the relations between the seven *gaddīs* and the conduct of *seva* across them.

Gokulanātha's role is the institutional consolidation of the second generation: the school's external apparatus (the *gaddīs*, the *vārtā*-tradition, the structures of *seva*) is fixed in the form it will be transmitted, and the doctrinal corpus is opened for second-generation teaching without the pitch of inter-school polemic that Puruṣottama is contemporaneously bringing to the *Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga*.

engaged_works.title: Caurasi-Vaishnavan-ki-Varta
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Redacted by Gokulanātha from oral and written hagiographical material assembled within the lineage; the standard edition fixes the corpus in the form he transmitted it. Some narratives are earlier than Gokulanātha; the redaction-frame and the doctrinal commentary are his.

ascription_evidence.description: Modern academic consensus in Puṣṭimārga textual scholarship.

engaged_works.summary: The *Caurāsī-Vaiṣṇavan-kī-Vārtā* is the Vraja-Bhāṣā compendium of eighty-four hagiographical narratives of Vallabha's principal disciples (*caurāsī* — eighty-four). Each narrative is structured as a doctrinal exemplum: the disciple's life, *bhāva*-mode, *seva*-discipline, encounter with Vallabha, and the moment of *anugraha* are narrated in a form that illustrates a specific point of *Puṣṭi-Mārga* doctrine in operation. The opening *avatārikā* (in the standard recension) fixes the editorial principle: each *vārtā* is to display *anugraha* operating against the *jīva*'s expected resistance — the doctrinal claim being that grace is genuinely independent of the *jīva*'s qualifications (*Vallabha, Puṣṭi-Pravāha-Maryādā-Bheda* on the *puṣṭi-jīva*'s status). Particular *vārtās* — those of Sūradās, Paramānandadās, Kṛṣṇadās, and Kumbhandās (the four principal *kīrtana*-poets, the *aṣṭa-chāp* core) — fix the lineage's reading of how the *Bhāgavata*-derived devotional aesthetics enter the *seva*-tradition. The *Vārtā* is doctrinally indistinguishable from Vallabha and Viṭṭhalanātha but operates in narrative-pastoral idiom, intended for daily reading by the lay *vaiṣṇava* community. It is the principal lineage-memory text of the first-generation disciples and the form in which most Puṣṭimārga *vaiṣṇavas* know that history.

engaged_works.title: Sarva-Nirnaya-Prakarana-Vivrti
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Vallabha's Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha.

ascription_evidence.description: Modern academic consensus in Puṣṭimārga textual scholarship.

engaged_works.summary: Gokulanātha's *Vivṛti* on the *Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa* — the third and longest of the three *prakaraṇas* of Vallabha's *Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha*, in which the metaphysics of *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* and the demarcation of *śuddhādvaita* against rival Vedānta is set out in compressed form. The *Vivṛti* opens Vallabha's compressed glosses verse by verse, fixing the school's reading of three load-bearing positions: the structure of *avikṛta-pariṇāma* (Brahman as the unmodifying material cause whose transformation is real but does not alter Brahman's *sat-cit-ānanda* nature, *Vivṛti* on *Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa* §§22–47); the rejection of *māyā* as *anirvacanīya* and the identification of *māyā* with Kṛṣṇa's own *śakti* (*Vivṛti* on §§48–66, against the *vivartavāda* of Padmapāda and Prakāśātman); the *jīva*'s status as real *aṃśa* with *ānanda* concealed (*Vivṛti* on §§67–95, against both Śaṅkara's identity-from-eternity and Madhva's strict *bheda*). The work operates at scholastic pitch sufficient for second-generation lineage teaching; it does not undertake the explicit inter-school polemic that Viṭṭhalanātha mounts in the *Vidvan-Maṇḍana* or that Puruṣottama mounts in the *Avaraṇa-Bhaṅga*. The *Vivṛti* is the standard sub-commentary on Vallabha's *Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa* in the lineage and is read alongside Hariraya's *Ṣoḍaśa-Granthāḥ-Vivaraṇa* in the school's curriculum prior to Puruṣottama's higher apparatus.
